rationale: The pair is likely substantive: the citing paper (“Sticky Priors,” 2004) centers on whether identity-based priors persist despite analytic/nonanalytic correction, while the cited paper (“Stickier Priors,” 2003) specifically develops the stickier-priors procedural-bias account and tests those same corrective tools (counterfactuals, accountability, decomposition). The strong conceptual and methodological overlap in the notes (nonanalytic vs analytic thinking, persistence after debiasing attempts) suggests the cited work is a probable theory/mechanism foundation rather than an incidental mention.
